description: Landing Page & Idea CRUD — Milestone 2. Implements the landing page with hero section, 4 ordered lists (My Ideas, Collaborating, Invitations, Trash), idea creation flow, search & filter, and floating ideas list. Provides full CRUD API for ideas with soft delete and restore.

userStories.title: Ideas REST API — CRUD Endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create, list, view, delete, and restore ideas via REST API so that the landing page and workspace can manage idea lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/ideas creates a new idea with first_message, returns 201 + idea object
GET /api/ideas lists user's ideas (owned + collaborating) with pagination
GET /api/ideas supports filter by ownership (my_ideas, collaborating, trash)
GET /api/ideas supports filter by state (open, in_review, accepted, dropped, rejected)
GET /api/ideas supports search by title (partial match, case-insensitive)
GET /api/ideas/:id returns full idea details for owner/collaborator, 403 for non-authorized
DELETE /api/ideas/:id sets deleted_at timestamp (soft delete), 200 response
POST /api/ideas/:id/restore clears deleted_at, 200 response
Unauthenticated requests return 401
Non-existent idea ID returns 404
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § ideas — id, title, state, visibility, owner_id, co_owner_id, deleted_at, created_at, updated_at. docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § idea_collaborators — join table for collaborating users. docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § POST /api/ideas, GET /api/ideas, GET /api/ideas/:id, DELETE /api/ideas/:id, POST /api/ideas/:id/restore — see request/response shapes, query params (filter, state, search, page, page_size), side effects (first_message creates ChatMessage, AI processing triggered).
Design: Not applicable (backend API only).
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § API-IDEA.01 (POST idea), API-IDEA.02 (empty first_message → 400), API-IDEA.03 (unauth → 401), API-IDEA.04 (GET list), API-IDEA.05 (filter=trash), API-IDEA.06 (GET by ID), API-IDEA.07 (non-owner → 403), API-IDEA.08 (not found → 404), API-IDEA.11 (soft delete), API-IDEA.12 (restore), T-9.3.01 (soft delete sets deleted_at), T-9.3.02 (restore clears deleted_at), T-9.4.01 (search by title), T-9.4.02 (filter by state) — all P1. Ralph must write these tests BEFORE implementing the endpoints (test-first). Each test includes: test ID, layer (Integration), description, input (HTTP request), expected output (status code + response shape or DB state).
Files: services/gateway/apps/ideas/views.py (ViewSet or APIView classes for all 5 endpoints), services/gateway/apps/ideas/serializers.py (IdeaSerializer, IdeaListSerializer with filters), services/gateway/apps/ideas/urls.py (router or urlpatterns), services/core/apps/ideas/services.py (business logic: create_idea, list_ideas, get_idea, soft_delete_idea, restore_idea), services/core/grpc_server/servicers/core_servicer.py (gRPC methods: CreateIdea, ListIdeas, GetIdea, SoftDeleteIdea, RestoreIdea), proto/core.proto (message definitions if not already present), services/gateway/apps/ideas/tests/test_views.py (API integration tests for all criteria).
Gotchas: Soft delete must SET deleted_at, not hard delete. Search by title uses ILIKE or icontains for case-insensitive partial match. Collaborating filter must join idea_collaborators table. Auth middleware already set up in M1 (request.user available). Use existing auth patterns from M1 (middleware ensures request.user is populated). Filter logic: my_ideas = owner_id = request.user.id, collaborating = idea_collaborators.user_id = request.user.id, trash = deleted_at IS NOT NULL.

data_model: ideas: id uuid PK, title varchar(500), state varchar(20) CHECK (open/in_review/accepted/dropped/rejected), visibility varchar(20), owner_id uuid FK→users, co_owner_id uuid FK→users (nullable), deleted_at timestamptz (nullable for soft delete), created_at, updated_at — see data-model.md § ideas
idea_collaborators: id uuid PK, idea_id uuid FK→ideas CASCADE, user_id uuid FK→users, joined_at timestamptz — UNIQUE (idea_id, user_id) — see data-model.md § idea_collaborators

chat_messages: id uuid PK, idea_id uuid FK→ideas CASCADE, sender_type varchar(10) CHECK (user/ai), sender_id uuid FK→users (nullable), content text, created_at — immutable — see data-model.md § chat_messages
api_endpoints: POST /api/ideas — req: {first_message: string} → 201 {id, title, state, visibility, agent_mode, owner, co_owner, created_at} + side effect: first ChatMessage created, AI processing triggered — see api-design.md § POST /api/ideas

GET /api/ideas — query: filter (my_ideas/collaborating/invitations/trash), state (open/in_review/etc), search (title partial match), page, page_size → 200 {results: [{id, title, state, visibility, role, owner, collaborator_count, inviter_display_name, updated_at, deleted_at}], count, next, previous} — see api-design.md § GET /api/ideas
GET /api/ideas/:id — → 200 full idea object or 403 ACCESS_DENIED if non-owner/non-collaborator or 404 NOT_FOUND — see api-design.md § GET /api/ideas/:id
DELETE /api/ideas/:id — sets deleted_at → 200 {message} — owner/co-owner only — see api-design.md § DELETE /api/ideas/:id
POST /api/ideas/:id/restore — clears deleted_at → 200 {message} — owner/co-owner only — see api-design.md § POST /api/ideas/:id/restore
test_cases: API-IDEA.01 | Integration | Create idea with first message | POST /api/ideas {first_message: 'My idea'} | 201 + idea object with id, title='', state='open'
API-IDEA.02 | Integration | Empty first_message validation | POST /api/ideas {first_message: ''} | 400
API-IDEA.03 | Integration | Unauthenticated request | POST /api/ideas without auth | 401
API-IDEA.04 | Integration | List user's ideas | GET /api/ideas | 200 + paginated results
API-IDEA.05 | Integration | Filter trash | GET /api/ideas?filter=trash | only trashed ideas (deleted_at IS NOT NULL)
API-IDEA.06 | Integration | Get idea as owner | GET /api/ideas/:id | 200 + full idea state
API-IDEA.07 | Integration | Non-owner access denied | GET /api/ideas/:id as non-owner/non-collaborator | 403 ACCESS_DENIED
API-IDEA.08 | Integration | Non-existent UUID | GET /api/ideas/invalid-uuid | 404 NOT_FOUND
API-IDEA.11 | Integration | Soft delete | DELETE /api/ideas/:id | 200, deleted_at set
API-IDEA.12 | Integration | Restore from trash | POST /api/ideas/:id/restore | 200, deleted_at null
T-9.3.01 | Integration | Soft delete moves to trash | DELETE /api/ideas/:id | deleted_at set, appears in trash list
T-9.3.02 | Integration | Restore clears deleted_at | POST /api/ideas/:id/restore | deleted_at = null
T-9.4.01 | Integration | Search by title | GET /api/ideas?search=keyword | filtered results returned
T-9.4.02 | Integration | Filter by state | GET /api/ideas?state=open | only open ideas returned
existing_code: services/gateway/apps/authentication/middleware.py: AuthenticationMiddleware.process_request() — sets request.user from session (dev bypass) or Azure AD JWT
services/core/apps/ideas/models.py: Idea model — full schema already exists from M1
services/core/apps/chat/models.py: ChatMessage model — full schema already exists from M1
proto/core.proto: CoreService definition — stub servicer already exists, add CreateIdea, ListIdeas, GetIdea, SoftDeleteIdea, RestoreIdea RPC methods

userStories.title: Invitations List API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to fetch my pending collaboration invitations via API so that the landing page can display them.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/invitations returns list of pending invitations for current user
Response includes invitation id, idea_id, idea_title, inviter display_name, created_at
Only pending invitations returned (status = 'pending')
Unauthenticated requests return 401
Empty list returned if no pending invitations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § collaboration_invitations — id, idea_id, inviter_id, invitee_id, status CHECK (pending/accepted/declined/revoked), created_at, responded_at. docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/invitations — returns array of pending invitations with inviter display_name.
Design: Not applicable (backend API only).
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-9.1.01 (Landing page renders all 4 lists including Invitations) — P1. The invitations list relies on this endpoint. Ralph must write an integration test for GET /api/invitations that verifies: authenticated request returns pending invitations with correct structure (id, idea_id, idea_title, inviter, created_at), only pending status, 401 for unauth.
Files: services/gateway/apps/collaboration/views.py (InvitationsListView or viewset), services/gateway/apps/collaboration/serializers.py (InvitationSerializer with nested inviter), services/gateway/apps/collaboration/urls.py (route for /api/invitations), services/core/apps/collaboration/services.py (list_pending_invitations business logic), services/core/grpc_server/servicers/core_servicer.py (ListPendingInvitations RPC), proto/core.proto (ListPendingInvitationsRequest, ListPendingInvitationsResponse messages), services/gateway/apps/collaboration/tests/test_views.py (integration tests).
Gotchas: Query must filter invitee_id = request.user.id AND status = 'pending'. Join ideas table to get idea_title. Join users table (via inviter_id) to get inviter display_name. Use existing auth middleware from M1.

data_model: collaboration_invitations: id uuid PK, idea_id uuid FK→ideas CASCADE, inviter_id uuid FK→users, invitee_id uuid FK→users, status varchar(15) CHECK (pending/accepted/declined/revoked) DEFAULT 'pending', created_at, responded_at (nullable) — see data-model.md § collaboration_invitations
ideas: id uuid PK, title varchar(500) — see data-model.md § ideas
users: id uuid PK, display_name varchar(300) — see data-model.md § users (Gateway-owned table)
api_endpoints: GET /api/invitations — → 200 {invitations: [{id, idea_id, idea_title, inviter: {id, display_name}, created_at}]} — see api-design.md § GET /api/invitations
test_cases: T-9.1.01 | Unit | Landing page renders all 4 lists | User with ideas in various states | My Ideas, Collaborating, Invitations, Trash lists rendered — invitations list requires GET /api/invitations to work
existing_code: services/core/apps/collaboration/models.py: CollaborationInvitation model — full schema already exists from M1
services/gateway/apps/authentication/middleware.py: AuthenticationMiddleware — provides request.user

userStories.title: Landing Page Layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want a landing page with a hero section and 4 ordered lists so that I can see all my ideas and invitations organized clearly.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section displays heading, subtext, creation input field, and submit button
4 ordered lists render: My Ideas, Collaborating, Invitations, Trash
Each list has a section heading with count
Empty states display appropriate messages for each list
Clicking an IdeaCard navigates to /idea/:uuid
Page uses PageShell layout with Navbar
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: Not applicable (frontend page only).
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § S1 Landing Page — hero section (centered heading 'Start a new brainstorm', subtext, creation input with 'Begin' button), 4 ordered lists (My Ideas → Collaborating → Invitations → Trash), each with heading + count badge, responsive (desktop multi-column grid, mobile single column stack). docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 11.3 Empty States — messages for each empty list (no ideas: 'Start your first brainstorm', no collaborations: contextual, no invitations: contextual, empty trash: 'Trash is empty').
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-9.1.01 (Landing page renders all 4 lists) — P1 Unit test. Ralph must render LandingPage component with mocked API responses (ideas, invitations) and assert all 4 list sections are present.
Files: frontend/src/pages/LandingPage/index.tsx (main page component with hero + 4 lists), frontend/src/components/landing/HeroSection.tsx (creation input + submit button), frontend/src/components/landing/IdeaCard.tsx (see US-004), frontend/src/components/landing/InvitationCard.tsx (invitation with accept/decline buttons), frontend/src/components/common/EmptyState.tsx (already exists from M1), frontend/src/i18n/locales/de.json and en.json (translation keys for landing page).
Gotchas: Lists are fetched separately (4 separate API calls) — My Ideas (GET /api/ideas?filter=my_ideas), Collaborating (GET /api/ideas?filter=collaborating), Invitations (GET /api/invitations), Trash (GET /api/ideas?filter=trash). Use TanStack Query for data fetching (install @tanstack/react-query if not present). All sections visible even if empty (use EmptyState component from M1 for empty lists). Hero section is M2; idea creation flow is US-005.

components: PageShell — Navbar + main content wrapper — already exists from M1, see layout/PageShell.tsx
EmptyState — icon + message + optional action — already exists from M1, see common/EmptyState.tsx
IdeaCard — see US-004 context
InvitationCard — gold left border, idea title, inviter name, accept/decline buttons — see component-inventory.md § InvitationCard
test_cases: T-9.1.01 | Unit | Landing page renders all 4 lists | User with ideas in various states | My Ideas, Collaborating, Invitations, Trash lists all rendered with section headings
existing_code: frontend/src/components/layout/PageShell.tsx: PageShell component — Navbar + children
frontend/src/components/common/EmptyState.tsx: EmptyState({icon, message, action}) — renders centered empty state with Lucide icon
frontend/src/hooks/use-auth.ts: useAuth() — provides {user, isAuthenticated, hasRole()} context

userStories.title: IdeaCard Component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see idea cards with state dot, title, timestamp, badge, and menu so that I can quickly understand and manage each idea.
acceptanceCriteria: State dot renders 8px circle with idea state color
Title displays with ellipsis truncation if too long
Timestamp shows relative time ('Last updated X ago')
State badge displays with correct state color and text
Three-dot menu shows Delete (for active) or Restore (for trash) option
Clicking card navigates to /idea/:uuid
Clicking three-dot menu does not trigger navigation
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: Not applicable (frontend component only).
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 2.2 Idea Card — state dot (8px circle, idea state color), title (Gotham Medium text-base, single-line truncate), timestamp (Gotham Book text-sm text-muted-foreground), state badge (see § 3.1), three-dot menu (ghost icon button, dropdown: Delete / Restore), selected state (gold left border border-l-4 border-primary). docs/03-design/design-system.md § 2.5 Idea State Colors — Open: sky-600 (light) / sky-400 (dark), In Review: amber-500, Accepted: green-600 / green-500, Dropped: gray-400, Rejected: orange-500.
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-9.1.01 (Landing page renders all 4 lists with IdeaCard components) — P1. Ralph must write a unit test for IdeaCard that renders with mock idea data and asserts: state dot color matches state, title displays, timestamp formats correctly, badge visible, three-dot menu triggers dropdown.
Files: frontend/src/components/landing/IdeaCard.tsx (card component with all elements), frontend/src/components/ui/badge.tsx (already exists from M1 with state variants), frontend/src/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx (already exists from M1), frontend/src/lib/utils.ts (helper: formatRelativeTime(date) → 'X ago').
Gotchas: Three-dot menu must stop event propagation (e.preventDefault() or stopPropagation()) to prevent card click navigation. State dot uses inline style with dynamic color. Badge component from M1 already has state variants (open, in_review, accepted, dropped, rejected). Title uses 'line-clamp-1' or 'truncate' utility for ellipsis. Timestamp formatting uses a helper function (not built-in Intl.RelativeTimeFormat — too verbose; use simple logic: < 1 min = 'just now', < 1 hour = 'X minutes ago', < 1 day = 'X hours ago', else = 'X days ago').

components: Badge — 8 variants including 5 idea states (open, in_review, accepted, dropped, rejected) — already exists from M1, see ui/badge.tsx
DropdownMenu — Radix dropdown with brand styling — already exists from M1, see ui/dropdown-menu.tsx
Card — base card with border, radius, shadow — already exists from M1, see ui/card.tsx
test_cases: T-9.1.01 | Unit | Landing page renders all 4 lists | includes IdeaCard components displaying state dot, title, timestamp, badge
existing_code: frontend/src/components/ui/badge.tsx: Badge component — variant prop includes 'open', 'in_review', 'accepted', 'dropped', 'rejected' for state badges
frontend/src/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx: DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuItem — Radix primitives
frontend/src/components/ui/card.tsx: Card, CardHeader, CardContent — base card components

userStories.title: Idea Creation Flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type a message in the hero section and create a new idea so that I can start brainstorming immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero input captures first message text
Submit button triggers POST /api/ideas with first_message
On success, redirect to /idea/:uuid (workspace page)
Loading state shows spinner on submit button during API call
Validation: empty input shows error message
Error toast displays on API failure with retry button
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § POST /api/ideas — req: {first_message: string} → 201 {id, ...} + side effects: first ChatMessage created, AI processing triggered.
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § S1.2 Landing Hero Section — centered heading 'Start a new brainstorm', subtext, creation input (large textarea, placeholder 'Describe your idea...'), primary button 'Begin'. docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 1.3 Button States — loading state with spinner, disabled when input empty.
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-9.2.01 (Create idea from landing page E2E test) — type message in hero, submit, verify redirect to workspace — P1. API-IDEA.01 (POST /api/ideas with first_message → 201) — P1 Integration test. Ralph must write both: E2E test using React Testing Library (render LandingPage, type in input, click submit, mock API response, assert navigation), and API integration test (verify POST endpoint returns 201).
Files: frontend/src/components/landing/HeroSection.tsx (input + submit button + error state + loading state), frontend/src/hooks/use-create-idea.ts (TanStack Query mutation for POST /api/ideas with onSuccess redirect), frontend/src/api/ideas.ts (createIdea(firstMessage) async function), frontend/src/i18n/locales/*.json (translation keys for hero section).
Gotchas: Use react-router-dom useNavigate() for redirect. TanStack Query mutation handles loading/error states. Empty input validation: disable button when input is empty (client-side), also show error toast if server returns 400. Textarea should be large (min-h-24) and auto-grow. Use Button component from M1 with loading prop (shows spinner). Error handling: use ErrorToast component from M1 with retry button that re-triggers mutation.

api_endpoints: POST /api/ideas — req: {first_message: string} → 201 {id, title, state, visibility, agent_mode, owner, co_owner, created_at} + side effect: first ChatMessage created — see api-design.md § POST /api/ideas
components: Button — loading state with spinner — already exists from M1, see ui/button.tsx
Textarea — auto-grow variant — already exists from M1, see ui/textarea.tsx
ErrorToast — persistent toast with retry button — already exists from M1, see common/ErrorToast.tsx
test_cases: T-9.2.01 | E2E | Create idea from landing page | Type message in hero section, click submit | New idea created (API returns 201), redirected to /idea/:uuid
API-IDEA.01 | Integration | Create idea with first message | POST /api/ideas {first_message: 'My idea'} | 201 + idea object with id
existing_code: frontend/src/components/ui/button.tsx: Button component — loading prop shows spinner, disabled when loading
frontend/src/components/ui/textarea.tsx: Textarea component — auto-grow variant available
frontend/src/components/common/ErrorToast.tsx: ErrorToast({message, onRetry}) — persistent error toast with retry button

userStories.title: Idea Lists with TanStack Query
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my ideas fetched and displayed in the 4 lists (My Ideas, Collaborating, Invitations, Trash) so that I can navigate to any idea or invitation.
acceptanceCriteria: My Ideas list fetches GET /api/ideas?filter=my_ideas and displays IdeaCard components
Collaborating list fetches GET /api/ideas?filter=collaborating
Invitations list fetches GET /api/invitations and displays InvitationCard components
Trash list fetches GET /api/ideas?filter=trash
Loading state shows skeleton loaders for each list
Empty state shows appropriate message for each list
Soft delete triggers undo toast with countdown
TanStack Query cache invalidated on create/delete/restore
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/ideas — query params: filter (my_ideas/collaborating/trash), returns paginated results. docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/invitations.
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § S1 Landing Page — 4 ordered lists with section headings, responsive grid (desktop: 2-column, mobile: single column). docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 11.2 Skeleton Loading — full card shape with 2 text lines. docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 11.1 Toast Notifications — undo toast for soft delete with countdown (5s auto-dismiss).
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-9.1.01 (all 4 lists render), T-9.3.01 (soft delete moves to trash), T-9.3.02 (restore clears deleted_at) — all P1. Ralph must write unit tests for: each list query hook (useMyIdeas, useCollaboratingIdeas, useInvitations, useTrash) mocked with test data, assert correct query key + endpoint called. Integration tests for soft delete and restore already covered in US-001.
Files: frontend/src/hooks/use-my-ideas.ts (useQuery for my_ideas), use-collaborating-ideas.ts, use-invitations.ts, use-trash.ts, frontend/src/api/ideas.ts (fetchIdeas, fetchInvitations async functions), frontend/src/components/landing/IdeasList.tsx (generic list component with loading/empty states), frontend/src/components/landing/InvitationCard.tsx (invitation card with accept/decline buttons — wired in M11, buttons render but not functional in M2).
Gotchas: TanStack Query queryClient.invalidateQueries() after mutation (create, delete, restore) to refresh all lists. Soft delete undo toast: use react-toastify with custom action button, 5s countdown, onClose triggers restore API call if undo clicked. Skeleton uses Skeleton component from M1. Empty states use EmptyState component from M1 with appropriate Lucide icons (Lightbulb, Users, Mail, Trash2).

api_endpoints: GET /api/ideas?filter=collaborating — returns ideas user collaborates on
GET /api/ideas?filter=trash — returns soft-deleted ideas (deleted_at IS NOT NULL)
GET /api/invitations — returns pending invitations for current user
components: IdeaCard — see US-004 context
InvitationCard — gold left border, idea title, inviter name, accept/decline buttons (render but not wired in M2)
Skeleton — pulse animation skeleton loader — already exists from M1, see ui/skeleton.tsx
EmptyState — icon + message — already exists from M1, see common/EmptyState.tsx
test_cases: T-9.1.01 | Unit | Landing page renders all 4 lists | User with ideas in various states | All 4 lists render with appropriate data
T-9.3.01 | Integration | Soft delete moves idea to trash | DELETE /api/ideas/:id | deleted_at set, idea appears in trash list
T-9.3.02 | Integration | Restore from trash | POST /api/ideas/:id/restore | deleted_at cleared, idea removed from trash list
existing_code: frontend/src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx: Skeleton component — animate-pulse placeholder
frontend/src/components/common/EmptyState.tsx: EmptyState({icon, message, action}) — renders empty state
frontend/src/components/ui/toast.tsx: Toast component (if exists) — or use react-toastify for undo toast with action button

userStories.title: Search & Filter Bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search ideas by title and filter by state/ownership so that I can quickly find specific ideas.
acceptanceCriteria: Search input filters ideas by title (partial match, case-insensitive)
State filter dropdown shows: All, Open, In Review, Accepted, Dropped, Rejected
Ownership filter dropdown shows: All, My Ideas, Collaborating
Search is debounced (300ms) to avoid excessive API calls
Filters apply to all lists except Invitations and Trash
Clear filters button resets all filters
Query params update in URL on filter change
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/ideas — query params: search (title partial match), state (open/in_review/etc), filter (my_ideas/collaborating) — all optional.
Design: docs/03-design/component-inventory.md § FilterBar — search input + state dropdown + ownership dropdown. docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 4.6 Search Input — Lucide Search icon (left), clear button (X icon, appears when input has value), debounce 300ms.
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-9.4.01 (search by title works), T-9.4.02 (filter by state works) — both P1 Integration tests. Ralph must also write unit tests for FilterBar component: render with mock onChange handlers, type in search (use vi.useFakeTimers to test debounce), select state dropdown, select ownership dropdown, assert onChange called with correct values.
Files: frontend/src/components/landing/FilterBar.tsx (search input + 2 dropdowns + clear button), frontend/src/hooks/use-ideas-filters.ts (custom hook managing filter state + URL sync), frontend/src/api/ideas.ts (fetchIdeas with query params).
Gotchas: Search debounce uses useDebounce hook or lodash debounce. URL sync: use react-router-dom useSearchParams() to read/write query params (?search=X&state=Y&filter=Z). Clear button visible only when any filter active. State dropdown uses Select component from M1. Ownership filter affects which endpoint: All = no filter param, My Ideas = filter=my_ideas, Collaborating = filter=collaborating. Filters do NOT apply to Trash list (always shows all trash). Invitations list has no filters.

api_endpoints: GET /api/ideas?search=keyword — partial match on title, case-insensitive
components: Input — Search variant with Lucide Search icon, clear button — see component-specs.md § 4.6
Select — Radix dropdown select — already exists from M1, see ui/select.tsx
Button — ghost variant for clear filters — already exists from M1, see ui/button.tsx
test_cases: T-9.4.01 | Integration | Search by title works | GET /api/ideas?search=keyword | filtered results returned
T-9.4.02 | Integration | Filter by state works | GET /api/ideas?state=open | only open ideas returned

frontend/src/components/ui/select.tsx: Select, SelectTrigger, SelectContent, SelectItem — Radix select primitives

userStories.title: Ideas List Floating Window
userStories.description: As a user, I want a floating window in the navbar showing my ideas grouped by state so that I can quickly navigate to any idea from anywhere in the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar button labeled 'Ideas' with icon
Clicking button opens floating window anchored to navbar
Floating window has 4 tabs: Active, In Review, Accepted, Closed
Active tab shows open ideas, In Review shows in_review ideas, etc.
Each tab shows compact IdeaCard items (title + state dot only)
Clicking idea navigates to /idea/:uuid and closes floating window
Empty tabs show 'No [state] ideas' message
Window closes on click outside or pressing Escape
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/ideas — query param state filters ideas. Four queries: state=open, state=in_review, state=accepted, state=dropped (Closed tab = dropped ideas).
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § S10 Ideas List Floating Window — navbar button 'Ideas', floating panel (320px wide, shadow-lg, rounded-lg), tabbed interface (Active, In Review, Accepted, Closed), compact idea items (state dot + title, no timestamp or badge). docs/03-design/component-inventory.md § IdeasListFloating — listed as Layout component (navbar-anchored floating panel).
Testing: No specific test IDs in test-matrix.md for this feature. Ralph must write unit tests: render IdeasListFloating component with mock data for each tab, assert tabs render, assert clicking idea navigates, assert window closes on Escape.
Files: frontend/src/components/layout/IdeasListFloating.tsx (floating window with tabs), frontend/src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx (add 'Ideas' button that toggles floating window), frontend/src/hooks/use-ideas-by-state.ts (TanStack Query hooks for each state filter), frontend/src/components/landing/IdeaCardCompact.tsx (minimal card: state dot + title only).
Gotchas: Floating window uses absolute positioning (z-index 20, below navbar which is z-40). Use Radix Popover or shadcn Popover component (not Dialog) for click-outside-to-close behavior. Tabs use Tabs component from M1. Each tab fetches independently (4 separate queries). Closed tab queries state=dropped (not state=closed, since 'dropped' is the actual enum value). Empty state uses EmptyState component from M1 with muted text only (no icon needed for compact empty state).

components: Tabs — horizontal tabs with gold underline — already exists from M1, see ui/tabs.tsx
IdeaCard — full card used in landing page lists — see US-004
IdeaCardCompact — minimal version: state dot + title only, no timestamp/badge/menu
EmptyState — muted text for empty tabs — already exists from M1, see common/EmptyState.tsx
existing_code: frontend/src/components/ui/tabs.tsx: Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent — Radix tabs primitives
frontend/src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx: Navbar component — add 'Ideas' button that toggles floating window
